answer: The algorithm determines “Estimated K mg/100g” values by finding the closest matching food entry in the English-Language Food Database of Compositions (EL FDC). This matching is based on comparing the four primary facets (B, C, E, F) of the YUM FDC food to those in the EL FDC. The algorithm prioritizes exact matches across all four facets. If an exact match is not found, it identifies the EL FDC entry with the most matching facets, particularly if the differing facets are closely related within the food classification charts (e.g., sharing a common parent category). The K value from this closest EL FDC match is then used as the estimated value.
